skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review web UI files against current Web Interface Guidelines, with checks for accessibility, UX, visual polish, and implementation details.
skill.summary: Audits web interface files against external design and accessibility guidelines.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto alerts were dismissed as Markdown false positives. The remaining confirmed issue is intentional network access to mutable remote guidelines, which is legitimate but should be disclosed as a medium supply-chain risk.

medium_findings.title: Mutable Remote Guideline Fetch
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to fetch guidelines from a hardcoded GitHub raw URL before each review. This is legitimate functionality, but remote content can change and influence review behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires a network fetch from a mutable URL and says the fetched content contains instructions. The use is expected for the skill, but it creates supply-chain and prompt-control risk.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Text
low_findings.description: The command-execution and weak-cryptography alerts are caused by Markdown backticks, a fenced URL, the word design, and a Git SHA reference. No executable Ruby backtick use or cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown metadata, inline code formatting, a URL code fence, and sync notes. They are not executable code paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Mutable Remote Guideline Fetch
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to fetch guidelines from a hardcoded GitHub raw URL before each review. This is legitimate functionality, but remote content can change and influence review behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires a network fetch from a mutable URL and says the fetched content contains instructions. The use is expected for the skill, but it creates supply-chain and prompt-control risk.

content.value_statement: Frontend reviews can miss accessibility, UX, and visual consistency issues. This skill applies current Web Interface Guidelines to selected files and returns concise findings.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the current Web Interface Guidelines from the configured GitHub raw URL.
Reviews user-specified files or file patterns for guideline compliance.
Checks interface code for accessibility, UX, and visual design issues.
Returns findings in a terse file and line oriented format.
Asks which files to review when no target is provided.
limitations: Requires network access to retrieve the latest external guidelines.
Depends on the availability and integrity of the remote GitHub content.
Does not include the guideline rules locally inside the skill package.
Review quality depends on the files and context supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge UI review
use_cases.description: Review changed frontend files before merging a pull request.

use_cases.title: Accessibility pass
use_cases.description: Check interface code for issues that affect keyboard, screen reader, and visual accessibility.

use_cases.title: Design consistency audit
use_cases.description: Find interface patterns that diverge from shared Web Interface Guidelines.

prompt_templates.title: Review one page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a single file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review app/page.tsx against the Web Interface Guidelines and list only actionable findings.

prompt_templates.title: Review changed components
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for a feature branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changed files in components/ for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Include file and line references.

prompt_templates.title: Audit accessibility risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit src/**/*.tsx for accessibility and interaction issues using the Web Interface Guidelines. Prioritize user-impacting problems.

prompt_templates.title: Review a full UI surface
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across several interface areas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the latest Web Interface Guidelines, review app/, components/, and styles/, then group findings by severity and affected workflow.

output_examples.input: Review components/CheckoutForm.tsx for Web Interface Guidelines compliance.
output_examples.output: components/CheckoutForm.tsx:42 Button label is ambiguous. Use specific action text that describes the submitted action.

output_examples.input: Check app/settings/page.tsx for accessibility and UX issues.
output: app/settings/page.tsx:18 Heading order skips a level and may confuse assistive technology.
app/settings/page.tsx:64 Toggle state is shown by color only. Add a text or icon state cue.

output_examples.input: Audit components/navigation for design guideline issues.
output_examples.output: components/navigation/Menu.tsx:31 Focus state is not visible enough for keyboard users.

best_practices: Provide the exact files or patterns that contain the UI under review.
Review changed files before broad audits to keep findings actionable.
Verify remote guideline updates before using findings for strict release gates.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad repository audits when only one UI workflow changed.
Do not treat guideline findings as replacements for manual usability testing.
Do not rely on this skill without network access to the guideline source.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews web interface files against external Web Interface Guidelines for accessibility, UX, and design consistency issues.

faq.question: Does it change my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is written to output findings, not to edit files automatically.

faq.question: Why does it need network access?
faq.answer: It fetches the latest guideline rules from a GitHub raw URL before each review.

faq.question: Can it review an entire application?
faq.answer: Yes, but targeted file patterns usually produce clearer and more useful findings.

faq.question: Does it include the guidelines locally?
faq.answer: No. The skill references a remote guideline document instead of bundling the rules.

faq.question: Is this suitable for release blocking?
faq.answer: Use it as one review signal. Confirm important findings with product, design, and accessibility review.
